segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They took stock fast because there was no slow left.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex: moving, rib-shot, functioning at perhaps 70%.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik: winded, uncut, checking his instruments with shaking hands.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen: whole in body, less in magic -- one vine-mark dim at the edge, blackened where she'd tried to touch the post-cast burn.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara: unmarked, a faint frost halo gone when she blinked.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan: coiled too tight, shadow obedient by force, four crescent marks pressed into his thigh through his trousers.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana: connection steadied, a tiny flicker when she turned to speak, lips still tasting copper from the split counsel.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin: vertical by stubbornness, the scale back to coal-glow, pain settling to the constant ache Elowen could ease later -- if it wasn't fresh-burn.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "They'll follow the Ember,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara said, breaking the silence.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Distance won't matter."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Surface,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex started -- then glanced toward the south, toward Millbrook and the scattered hamlets tucked under these ridges. Her jaw tightened.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "No. Not with a beacon on two legs. I hate the idea, but... containment."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "The Undervault,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik offered, already pulling the battered journal from his coat. Pages crackled, stained with old blood and older ink.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Pre-Concord passages. Old dwarf-wards. My grandfather mapped entries and marked the Deep Hall. Wards there might confuse whatever is sniffing us."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Safer for civilians,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said, jaw set.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Not for us."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen's gaze went inward, the way it did when she listened to the Weave. The green of her tattoos pulsed faintly, then dimmed.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "The deep places are hurt. Wounded things can be generous. Or angry."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan's fingers twitched toward a blade, then away -- a motion so fast only Vex caught it.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Safer to be hunted in stone than among farms."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They looked to Caelin.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale warmed -- not push, not pull. Waiting.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He thought of Thornwick. Of glass bones where houses had stood. Of Mira's small hand clutching his ruined cloak.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Undervault,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "We don't bring this down to doorsteps."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Excellent,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said, already shouldering weight, adjusting straps with practiced efficiency.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I've always wanted to risk my life on decayed ventilation and ancestral pride."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Lead on,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said, checking her daggers with methodical precision.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "If a ward eats us, I'm haunting you."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Queue forms to the left,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said, finding his cheer again despite everything.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Ancestral disapproval is first-come, first-served."
